params.prompt: Vertical portrait brand social tile for a barbershop, exact 4:5 aspect ratio, 1080x1350 Instagram feed post. Background: high-contrast black and white editorial photography, duotone grading with a warm golden-yellow rim/key light used only as an implied light source, never a flat color wash, no straight color photography. Scene: close-up of a man's hands being massaged with moisturizing cream by a technician's gloved hands, glistening highlight on the skin from the implied warm key light, folded white towel beneath the hands, calm and deliberate motion, moody barbershop interior softly blurred behind, tight confident hand-detail crop, no faces needed. Add a dark ink (#101010) gradient scrim across the lower half for text legibility. Top-left: a small solid Nordic-yellow (#FFD200) pill-shaped tag containing bold condensed uppercase sans-serif text "PASO 03/04" in dark ink letters, tight letter tracking. Center-lower area: a large headline reading "HIDRATACION PROFUNDA", set in the hand-cut marker lettering style shown in the second reference image (a font character map) -- reproduce that exact letterform style, all uppercase, tight line height -- where the word PROFUNDA is solid Nordic yellow (#FFD200) and the rest of the word is solid bone-white (#F7F4EC), no other colors on the headline. Directly below the headline, one short line of clean condensed grotesk sans-serif body text in bone-white at reduced opacity, reading: "Sella la limpieza con hidratacion, o parafina si quieres ir por mas." Bottom-right corner: place the exact logo lockup shown in the first reference image (a white Viking helmet mark beside the words NORDIC / Barber Shop, already on a black background) completely unchanged and at small watermark scale -- do not invent, redraw, alter proportions, recolor, or add glow/shadow to this logo; blend its black background seamlessly into the tile's own ink-black canvas so no visible box or seam appears around it. Keep the overall canvas ink-black (#101010) dominant, yellow only as a small accent never exceeding about 10-15% of the frame.
